steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Information and Ideas question using data from a table. The task is to complete the claim about why Chang‘e 5 samples are significant. The decisive clue is the age data: Chang‘e 5 samples are approximately 2.0 billion years old, while Apollo 11, 15, and 17 samples are 3.6, 3.3, and 3.8 billion years old respectively — making Chang‘e 5 samples at least 1.3 billion years younger than any Apollo sample.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘they are much younger than the samples brought back from any of the Apollo missions.’: Directly supported by the table: 2.0 billion years vs. 3.3–3.8 billion years for all three Apollo missions. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘they were collected from the same landing site as the Apollo 11 mission.’: The table shows Chang‘e 5 landed at Oceanus Procellarum; no Apollo mission is shown with that landing site. Contradicted by the data. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘they are closest in age to the samples brought back by the Apollo 17 mission.’: Apollo 17 samples are 3.8 billion years old; Apollo 15 samples are 3.3 billion years old. Chang‘e 5 (2.0 billion) is closer in age to Apollo 15, not Apollo 17. Contradicted by the data. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘they helped confirm the predicted ages of the lunar samples from the Apollo missions.’: Neither the passage nor the table contains any information about predicted ages or confirmation of Apollo sample ages. Unsupported. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
